page: Microsoft Azure M32bds_v3 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the MBDSMediumMemoryv3 family. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 8473C processor with an x86_64 architecture, featuring 16 physical cores and 32 dedicated vCPUs operating at a clock speed of 4.0 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 256.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 8.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. It features a 105 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as Intel Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated matrix operations.

This server is deployed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor and includes 410 GB of local storage. It is premium storage capable, allowing integration with high-performance block storage options. The system does not include any dedicated GPU hardware, relying entirely on the host CPU for compute tasks.

Benchmark data indicates highly competitive single-core performance, with Geekbench single-core and text processing scores placing in the top 10% tier. Multi-core performance is also robust, with Geekbench multi-core, image processing, and bzip3 decompression benchmarks ranking in the top 25%. In terms of memory performance, the system achieves a read-only cached small-block bandwidth of 2766.64 GB/sec and an uncached large-block write bandwidth of 86.33 GB/sec. Large-model LLM inference represents a performance bottleneck, scoring in the bottom 25%, while small-model text generation performs strongly in the top 25%.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the M32bds_v3 offers a high-density memory-to-CPU ratio suitable for memory-intensive enterprise workloads without the overhead of GPU hardware. It is well-suited for relational databases, in-memory caching systems, text processing pipelines, software compilation, and medium-scale web serving.
description: Microsoft Azure M32bds_v3 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the MBDSMediumMemoryv3 family, designed for memory-intensive workloads. Powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8473C processor running at 4.0 GHz, it features 16 physical cores, 32 vCPUs, and 256.0 GB of RAM, providing a 1:8 vCPU-to-memory ratio. The instance includes 410 GB of local storage and is premium storage capable, though it lacks GPU acceleration. Performance benchmarks highlight top-tier single-core and text processing capabilities, alongside strong multi-core and decompression speeds. However, large-scale LLM inference is a notable bottleneck. This server is highly suited for database operations, in-memory caching, text processing, and general-purpose enterprise applications requiring high memory density.
og_description: Microsoft Azure M32bds_v3 is a memory-optimized server featuring 32 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and a 4.0 GHz Intel Xeon processor, delivering top-tier single-core performance for databases and memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure M32bds_v3 is a memory-optimized server with 32 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 410 GB local storage, ideal for database and memory-heavy workloads.
tagline: A high-frequency, memory-optimized virtual server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processing and balanced local storage for demanding enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8473C processor with 32 dedicated vCPUs running at 4.0 GHz
256.0 GB of RAM providing a high-density 1:8 vCPU-to-memory ratio
410 GB of local storage with premium block storage compatibility
Top-tier single-core and text processing benchmark performance
Optimized for relational databases, in-memory caching, and enterprise applications
